Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after pressing RIGHT. The character seems to have moved closer to the divider. Let me compare the before and after frames to see exactly what moved.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

# Find the character node (black with white dots)
def find_char(nodes):
    for n in nodes:
        if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] > 20 and len(n['children']) > 0:
            return n
    return None

char_before = find_char(before)
char_after = find_char(after)

if char_before and char_after:
    b_before = char_before['boundary']
    b_after = char_after['boundary']
    rows_b = [p[0] for p in b_before]
    cols_b = [p[1] for p in b_before]
    rows_a = [p[0] for p in b_after]
    cols_a = [p[1] for p in b_after]
    print(f"Character moved from ({min(rows_b)},{min(cols_b)})-({max(rows_b)},{max(cols_b)}) to ({min(rows_a)},{min(cols_a)})-({max(rows_a)},{max(cols_a)})")
    print(f"Delta: row {min(rows_a)-min(rows_b)}, col {min(cols_a)-min(cols_b)}")

# Check all nodes for changes
print("\nAll node positions after RIGHT:")
for n in after:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After pressing RIGHT:
- The character (black with white dots) moved right by 3 columns (from col 18 to col 21)
- But also the dark gray object (Node 5/6) moved from (15,36)-(23,44) to (15,33)-(23,41) - also shifted left by 3!
- The yellow bar on the right edge changed from Node 3 (Y, 64 pixels) to Node 4 (Y, 63 pixels) - seems like it shrank by 1 pixel
- The bottom black bar (Node 12/13) stayed at row 63
Wait, let me re-examine. The dark object moved from col 36-44 to col 33-41, that's a shift of -3 (left). The character moved +3 (right). This seems like everything shifted - the character moved right by 3, and the dark object on the right panel moved left by 3.
Actually, looking more carefully at the image, it seems like the character is now touching the sky blue divider. The character moved right 3 cells. But the dark gray object also seems to have moved.
Wait - let me reconsider. Maybe this isn't a simple movement game. Let me check the right panel objects too.
Actually, looking at the node IDs, they shifted. Let me look at this more carefully:
- Before: Node 5 was charcoal (c), bbox (15,36)-(23,44) 
- After: Node 6 is charcoal (c), bbox (15,33)-(23,41) - shifted left by 3
So both the character and the dark gray object moved. The character moved right 3, the dark object moved left 3. They're moving toward each other!
The yellow bar at col 63 seems to have changed - it went from 64 pixels to 63 pixels (shrank by 1). This could be a timer/HUD.
Let me try more RIGHT presses to see if the character keeps moving and what happens when it hits the divider.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try pressing RIGHT again to see the pattern
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
